ud6.10:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud6.10:1.3: Then Ven. Ānanda went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
ud6.10:1.4: As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
ud6.10:2.1:
“Lord, as long as Tathāgatas–worthy & rightly self-awakened–
do not appear in the world, that’s when the wanderers of other sects are worshipped,
revered, honored, venerated, and given homage–recipients of robes,
alms food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick.
ud6.10:2.3:
Now only the Blessed One is worshipped, revered, honored, venerated, and given homage–a recipient of robes,
alms food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick–along with the community of monks.”
ud6.10:3.1:
“That’s how it is, Ānanda.
That’s how it is.
As long as Tathāgatas–worthy & rightly self-awakened–do not appear in the world,
that’s when the wanderers of other sects are worshipped,
revered, honored, venerated, and given homage–recipients of robes,
alms food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick.
ud6.10:3.3:
Now only the Tathāgata is worshipped, revered, honored, venerated, and given homage–a recipient of robes,
alms food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick–along with the community of monks.”
ud6.10:4.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud6.10:5.2: as long as the sun hasn’t risen.
ud6.10:5.4:
the glowworm’s light is destroyed.
It no longer shines.
ud6.10:6.2: as long as those rightly awakened don’t appear in the world.
ud6.10:6.3: Those logicians aren’t purified, nor are their disciples.
ud6.10:6.4: Those of bad views aren’t released from stress.
